skippedSources.name: BowTieBull
skippedSources.reason: Career/life essay with no extractable market data or tweet angles

items.title: Saylor sold Bitcoin for only the 2nd time — and there's a BTC price where MSTR equity hits $0
items.idea: Strategy sold 32 BTC for $2.5M — only the second time its Bitcoin balance has nominally declined (the first was 704 BTC in Dec 2022). With 843,706 BTC on the balance sheet, CheckOnChain argues there is a mathematically calculable Bitcoin price at which MSTR's residual equity value goes to zero. The stock trades at a massive premium to its BTC NAV — but that premium compresses as BTC falls, and at some threshold flips negative.
items.angle: Combined with BTC now down 45% from its $120K peak, this is a risk management story hiding inside a balance sheet. The 'never sell' narrative was MSTR's brand moat. Cracking it — even by 0.0038% — does psychological damage that outweighs the fundamental size of the sale.
items.tweetA: Strategy broke its 'never sell' rule. 32 BTC. $2.5M. 0.0038% of holdings. Fundamentally irrelevant. Psychologically catastrophic. When your entire brand equity is 'we never sell Bitcoin,' the moment you do — even to fund a dividend — you've changed what you are. $MSTR
items.tweetB: There is a specific Bitcoin price at which MSTR's equity value is mathematically $0. CheckOnChain just ran the math. With BTC down 45% from $120K and Strategy selling for the first time since 2022, now is the time to understand what that number is before you find out the hard way.

items.title: Deutsche Bank's 3-act May: Brent -19.3%, semis +81.5% YTD, 30Y hit 5.18% post-2007 high
items.idea: Henry Allen frames May as a 3-act market drama. Act 1: geopolitics drove Brent crude down 19.3% — its largest monthly decline since the March 2020 pandemic crash. Act 2: Philly Semiconductor Index +22.2% in May alone (+81.5% YTD); KOSPI surged 28.5% in May, pushing YTD gains above 100% in local currency. Act 3: structural tension as 30yr Treasury hit 5.18% (post-2007 high May 19), 10yr Bund hit 3.19% (post-2011 high), Japan 10yr hit 2.78% (post-1997 high).
items.angle: The intra-month reversal in yields is the tell: bond markets are as volatile as the geopolitical newsflow. The AI trade absorbed a 19% oil crash, multi-decade yield spikes, and geopolitical whiplash. That 'until it can't' is the question for the second half.
items.tweetA: May in four numbers: Brent -19.3% (worst since pandemic). Philly Semis +22.2%. 30yr Treasury 5.18% (post-2007 high). KOSPI +100% YTD in local currency. Same month. Same planet. Different markets. The AI gravity well is so strong it bent every other asset class around it.
items.tweetB: The 30yr hit 5.18%, German bunds hit post-2011 highs, Japan yields hit post-1997 highs — all in May. Then they all reversed as Iran deal optimism returned. Bond markets are now fully hostage to the same geopolitical headlines as oil. This is not a normal rate environment.

items.title: SpaceX/xAI, OpenAI, Anthropic — all three filed for IPO simultaneously. Goldman leads all three.
items.idea: Three simultaneous mega-AI IPOs are in process, each with $1T+ expected market caps. Anthropic confidentially filed Monday after raising $65B at a $900B pre-money valuation — surpassing OpenAI's $730B last valuation. Goldman Sachs is left-leading all three deals with separate internal teams.
items.angle: Goldman running three simultaneous $1T+ IPO books is operationally unprecedented. SpaceX prices first (June 11-12), setting the risk premium benchmark. If SpaceX trades well, Anthropic and OpenAI get a tailwind. Watch SpaceX's first day like a macro indicator.
items.tweetA: Goldman Sachs is simultaneously leading the IPOs of SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic — three separate internal teams. Each expected at $1T+ market caps. Nothing like this has happened in capital markets history. The firm that IPOs the internet is IPOing AI.
items.tweetB: Anthropic raised $65B at $900B pre-money. That's higher than OpenAI's $730B last round. Last week OpenAI was the most valuable AI company. This week Anthropic is. And both just filed for IPO. SpaceX prices first — June 11-12. Everything rides on that first day of trading.

items.title: OpenAI + Oracle break ground on $45B StarGate 1GW Michigan campus — agentic AI is the demand driver
items.idea: OpenAI and Oracle broke ground on the StarGate data center in Saline Township, Michigan. Initial infrastructure phase $16B; full project $45B+. Sam Altman explicitly framed the demand shift: compute is moving from query-response to persistent agentic systems running 24/7 — handling documents, Slack threads, and workflows — a qualitative leap in compute-per-user. Oracle CEO Safra Catz: 'Customer demand now exceeds what Oracle can currently deliver.'
items.angle: Agentic AI demand is the new justification for the next order of magnitude in capex. Background agents running 24/7 vs on-demand queries could be a 100x compute multiplier per user. Every data center and power thesis just got a new tailwind.
items.tweetA: Sam Altman just explained why $45B on one Michigan data center makes sense: AI is moving from 'respond to queries' to 'run in the background 24/7 handling your documents and Slack.' That's not a 10x increase in compute demand. It's potentially 100x. $ORCL $NVDA
items.tweetB: Oracle CEO Safra Catz on StarGate: 'Customer demand for AI capacity now exceeds what Oracle can currently deliver.' On a $45B data center. That's not a supply problem. That's a signal that the demand curve is vertical. Power, cooling, fiber — all of it still undersupplied.

items.title: The de-equitisation era ends: SpaceX IPO wave could flood the market with supply by 2027-28
items.idea: Goldman's Ben Snider documents that corporate equity demand (buybacks + dividends) has exceeded supply every year since 2003. S&P 500 divisor peaked at dot-com bubble and has declined 23 years. Companies with <7% float at IPO see 54% of shares in circulation two years later. If SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI follow historical lockup-expiry patterns, new equity supply becomes potentially 'unbearable' in 2027-2028. Historical parallel: dot-com IPO peak 1999, crash hit 2000 after lockups expired.
items.angle: The setup rhymes with 1999-2000 in a specific and testable way. SpaceX June 2026 + lockups expiring late 2027 = pencil in the supply shock for 2027. Alphabet also plans to raise $80B separately.
items.tweetA: Goldman's research: corporate buybacks have exceeded new equity issuance EVERY SINGLE YEAR since 2003. That 23-year structural tailwind for stocks is ending now. SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic, plus Alphabet raising $80B = de-equitisation in reverse. Dot-com peak was 1999. Lockups expired 2000.
items.tweetB: Companies with <7% float at IPO historically have 54% of their shares in circulation 2 years later. SpaceX prices in June 2026. Do the math for June 2028. Three $1T companies hitting their lockup expiry in the same window could be the biggest equity supply shock in a generation.

charts.desc: 4 Goldman charts: IPO volume drought; buyback vs issuance; S&P divisor declining since 2003; float expansion post-IPO. CHART WORTH SAVING.

items.title: Bitcoin down 45% from $120K peak — Coinbase volume down 66%. Everything is being sold to buy semis.
items.idea: Bitcoin was above $120,000 just seven months ago; now struggling to hold $80,000 (-45%). ByteTree's Charlie Morris: 'It's semis or nothing, and so everything's been sold — quality value stocks, bonds, gold, Bitcoin — everything's being sold to semis.' Coinbase 30-day average BTC/USD volume peaked at $1.33B in February (BTC ~$60K) and has fallen 66% since. FRNT Financial sees 'tepid leverage and risk demand in crypto.'
items.angle: Bitcoin is not failing on its own merits — it's losing a capital allocation competition to semiconductors. When the AI trade peaks, the question is whether crypto gets its capital back or whether it flows to bonds and value first.
items.tweetA: Charlie Morris: 'It's semis or nothing. Everything is being sold to the semis. Bonds aren't happy. Gold isn't happy. Bitcoin isn't happy.' BTC volume on Coinbase is down 66% from its Feb peak. This isn't a crypto crisis. It's a capital rotation. The trade is: when do semis peak?
items.tweetB: Bitcoin was above $120,000 seven months ago. It's at $80,000 today. Down 45%. Not because of regulation, not because of hacks — because a handful of semiconductor stocks are better. When semis eventually correct, $BTC gets its capital back. Or it doesn't. That's the whole question.

charts.desc: BTC vs Middle East news events; BTC vs Philly Semi divergence. CHART WORTH SAVING.

items.title: US manufacturing ISM at 4-year high — may force a rate reassessment just as 30Y hits 5%
items.idea: Monday's ISM Manufacturing survey came in surprisingly strong — fastest expansion in four years. Dean Chen of Bitunix argues the key driver 'is no longer whether the Fed will raise rates again, but whether the bond market has already delivered the effects of another rate hike before policymakers act.' If Friday's payrolls and May CPI confirm a strong economy, expect renewed pressure on Treasury yields already above 5% on the 30yr.
items.angle: Strong manufacturing + strong payrolls + PCE 3.8% = the bond market does the Fed's job for it. Every basis point the 30Y stays above 5% is equivalent to an incremental Fed hike without a vote.
items.tweetA: US manufacturing ISM just printed at its fastest expansion in 4 years. PCE is 3.8%. The 30yr is above 5% for the first time since 2007. The Fed doesn't need to hike — the bond market is already doing it. Every day the 30Y stays here, it's like a shadow rate hike nobody voted on.
items.tweetB: The question for equities isn't whether Warsh hikes. It's whether the bond market — already at 5% on the 30yr — has already priced in what a hike would do. Stock-bond correlation is at a 20-year high. Which means if bonds sell off on strong payrolls Friday, stocks go with them. No safe haven.

items.title: Nvidia enters PC processor market — Qualcomm -9.6%, Intel -5.8%, Dell +10%, HP +8%
items.idea: Jensen Huang announced a new Nvidia PC processor at GTC Taipei (Computex). Qualcomm fell 9.6% and Intel fell 4-5.8% as PC chip incumbents were directly threatened. Dell +10% and HP +8% on AI-PC refresh cycle thesis. Cisco raised its AI orders pipeline from $5B to $9B. NVDA-Anthropic partnership described by Huang as 'scaling very, very quickly.'
items.angle: The PC market was the last hardware segment Nvidia hadn't disrupted. Now it has. The AI-PC refresh cycle across ~1.5B installed PCs becomes a new multi-year earnings revision story for Dell, HP, and NVDA itself.
items.tweetA: Nvidia just entered the PC processor market. One announcement: Qualcomm -9.6%, Intel -5.8%. The last hardware market Jensen Huang hadn't taken is now his target. $NVDA is no longer just the AI data center company. It's the PC company, the networking company, and the robotics company.
items.tweetB: Dell +10%, HP +8% on Nvidia's PC chip announcement. There are ~1.5 billion PCs installed globally due for replacement. If Nvidia's PC chip makes AI-native PCs the standard, Dell and HP just became AI hardware refresh plays. This cycle hasn't started yet. $DELL $HPQ $NVDA

items.title: Iran suspended US talks; Trump says deal 'reachable in a week'; Hormuz still closed — oil $89-93
items.idea: Iranian state media announced Tehran suspended talks over Israel's Lebanon actions; Trump said Hormuz reopening and ceasefire extension were 'reachable over the next week'; CNN's regional source said negotiations were 'back on track.' Trump sent back edits demanding tougher nuclear + Hormuz language. Hormuz remains effectively closed (~3 months of conflict since Feb 28), normally handling ~20% of global oil and LNG flows. Analysts note even a deal means slow flow recovery (mine clearing, infrastructure repairs, shut-in well restarts).
items.angle: The market keeps pricing 'deal = immediate supply return.' But 20% of global oil flows don't come back overnight after 3 months of conflict. The post-deal supply recovery lag is the underpriced asymmetry.
items.tweetA: Iran 'suspends talks.' Trump says deal 'reachable in a week.' CNN says it's 'back on track.' Three contradictory Iran headlines in one morning. Hormuz has been closed for 3 months. Even a deal doesn't flip the switch — mine clearing, repairs, shut-in restarts take months. The market keeps pricing instant recovery. It won't be.
items.tweetB: Buried in the Iran noise: even if a Hormuz deal happens this week, oil flow recovery will be SLOW — mines, repairs, well restarts. The market keeps pricing 'deal = immediate supply return.' 20% of global flows don't come back overnight. The oil vol trade is asymmetric and the market is getting the fundamentals wrong.

items.title: April PCE 3.8% YoY (highest since 2023); 30Y above 5%; stock-bond correlation at 20-year high
items.idea: April PCE 3.8% YoY — highest since 2023 — while savings rate dropped. The 30-year Treasury crossed 5% (first time since 2007). Stock-bond correlation hit a 20-year high, meaning bonds are not hedging equities. The traditional 60/40 hedge is broken. Kevin Warsh is in Day 10 as Fed Chair; first FOMC is June 16-17.
items.angle: A 20-year high in stock-bond correlation means every institutional portfolio is more exposed than its risk model suggests. With Warsh at the Fed, PCE at 3.8%, and the 30Y at 5%, the probability of simultaneous equity + bond selloff is higher than consensus.
items.tweetA: Stock-bond correlation is at a 20-year high. That means the 60/40 portfolio is broken. Bonds are not hedging equities. They're falling with them. PCE at 3.8%. 30yr at 5%. Warsh at the Fed with his first meeting in 2 weeks. The timing is not comfortable.
items.tweetB: PCE 3.8% is the highest since 2023. Savings rate dropped. 30Y above 5% for the first time since 2007. And the S&P is at record highs. Either the bond market is wrong, or the equity market is wrong. With stock-bond correlation at a 20-year high, you don't get to be right on both.

items.title: Spot BTC ETFs: 10 consecutive outflow days — first time EVER. $2.97B drained. SOL inflows diverge.
items.idea: BTC spot ETFs logged 10 consecutive outflow days through May 29 — first such streak since launch. AUM dropped from $104.29B to $94.17B ($2.97B drained). ETH ETFs on a 14-session outflow streak (~$2.6B drained). Structural outlier: Solana ETFs had zero outflow days all of May with $115.34M inflows (4th positive week). Glassnode flags extreme outflow streaks historically coincide with local BTC price bottoms. BTC key support: $72,500-73,000 then $68,300.
items.angle: SOL zero outflow days while BTC and ETH bled is the cleanest institutional rotation signal in crypto. Institutions are not leaving crypto — they're concentrating into Solana.
items.tweetA: Spot BTC ETFs just posted their first-ever 10-day outflow streak. $2.97B gone. ETH ETFs on a 14-day streak. Meanwhile: Solana ETFs had ZERO outflow days in May. $115M of inflows. Institutions aren't leaving crypto. They're rotating out of BTC and ETH and into SOL. That's the trade.
items.tweetB: BTC ETF AUM: $104B → $94B in two weeks. First 10-day outflow streak ever. But Glassnode notes streaks this extreme have historically marked local bottoms. Support at $72,500-73,000 then $68,300. Is this paper hands leaving, or real holders? That question determines the next $20K move.

items.title: South Korea: first glimpse of AI-driven inflation forcing central bank tightening — BoK to hike in July
items.idea: South Korea's KOSPI has surged 100%+ YTD in local currency — the world's purest AI equity trade. May inflation broadened while activity remained resilient, forcing the Bank of Korea toward tightening: expected 25bps hike in July and October, further hikes in H1 2027. Japan's BoJ continues normalization. When both BoK and BoJ are hiking alongside a hawkish Fed, 'it starts to look less like isolated policy adjustments and more like the early stages of a broader tightening cycle.'
items.angle: KOSPI +100% YTD = most extreme AI bubble pricing in public equity markets anywhere. Forced tightening in Korea is not priced in. Watch BoK July meeting as the global AI liquidity canary.
items.tweetA: South Korea: KOSPI +100% YTD. Also South Korea: Bank of Korea expected to hike rates in July, October, and H1 2027. The world's most levered AI equity trade is about to face monetary tightening at home. The balloon doesn't burst — it slowly loses altitude as liquidity conditions change.
items.tweetB: Korea, Japan, and the Fed are all tightening or hawkish simultaneously. The AI trade had geopolitical cover stories for why yields were rising. But when BoK hikes in July, there's no cover. It'll be explicit: the AI boom itself is causing the inflation that will eventually cool it.

items.title: Goldman raises copper to $13,735/tonne; Citi sees $15,000/tonne — sulphuric acid is the wildcard
items.idea: Goldman raised end-2026 LME copper target from $12,465 to $13,735/tonne ($6.25/lb); Citi to $14,500 this month and $15,000 within a year. US copper imports doubled YoY in Q1 2026 to 533,000 tonnes. China's May 1 sulphuric acid export ban + Hormuz logistics stress threatens SX-EW copper production (~17% of global supply). Goldman cut 2026 mine supply outlook by 350,000 tonnes (Grasberg + Kamoa-Kakula, neither back to full capacity until 2028).
items.angle: The acid supply story is not in consensus copper models. SX-EW copper running on acid supply that is now constrained by two independent shocks (China ban + Hormuz). Goldman is already force-revising upward. Citi's $15,000 would be a new ATH by a wide margin.
items.tweetA: Goldman raised copper to $13,735/tonne. Citi is at $15,000 within a year. But nobody is talking about sulphuric acid. China banned acid exports May 1. Hormuz logistics stress is crimping acid shipments. SX-EW copper — 17% of global supply — runs on acid. Do the math on what a prolonged Hormuz closure does to copper.
items.tweetB: US copper imports more than doubled YoY in Q1 2026. Goldman cut 2026 mine supply by 350,000 tonnes. Grasberg and Kamoa-Kakula won't be back to full capacity until 2028. Copper is now strategic — grid, defense, AI data centers, reshoring. And the world has less of it than models expected. $FCX $HBM

charts.desc: Copper weekly price chart approaching ATH; FCX daily; HBM daily. CHART WORTH SAVING.

items.title: North American copper M&A thesis: Hudbay's $1.5B Arizona deal is just the beginning
items.idea: Hudbay Minerals acquired Arizona Sonoran Copper for $1.5B at an all-time high share price valuation — acknowledging scarcity of Tier-1 copper assets in safe North American jurisdictions. S&P Global confirms major copper discoveries remain scarce; 'major copper discoveries in the USA are like finding a unicorn.' North American assets now benefit from government support: grid security financing, infrastructure funding, critical minerals partnerships, national security supply chain policy. Colombia presidential election runoff adds a new variable for Americas mining.
items.angle: We are in the first innings of government-backed North American critical minerals M&A. The names with late-stage copper assets in Arizona, Nevada, and British Columbia are being re-rated as strategic rather than cyclical. Premium on premium if M&A accelerates.
items.tweetA: Hudbay paid all-time-high valuation to acquire Arizona Sonoran Copper. Why? Because major copper discoveries in the USA are 'like finding a unicorn.' We're in the first inning of government-backed North American critical minerals M&A. The premium for jurisdiction is just starting to get priced in.
items.tweetB: The copper M&A thesis: Tier-1 copper in safe jurisdictions is scarce. AI data centers + EV grid + defense + reshoring all need copper. North American assets get government support. Hudbay's $1.5B Arizona deal is the first trade of the cycle. There will be more. Also watch Argentina — new mining-friendly backdrop attracting capital.

items.title: SAP fell 37% despite 9% revenue growth — cloud backlog decel spooked market; AI monetization is the unlock
items.idea: SAP at $196, down 37% from $313 ATH (July 2025). Revenue growing 5 consecutive years; operating margins 27.4% LTM. 90%+ of Fortune 500 use SAP; 80% of legacy customers committed to S/4HANA; RPO +92% in 3 years; switching costs up to $700M. The stock fell 22% in January when cloud backlog growth came in at 26% with guidance for 'slight deceleration.' FY26 estimates: revenue +13.2% YoY, EPS +19.2% YoY; FY27 fwd PE 19.8x. Jensen Huang named SAP as an enterprise AI agent partner at GTC Taipei. AI shift: moving from seat-based to consumption-based pricing (charging for outcomes).
items.angle: SAP is mission-critical ERP with Fortune 500 lock-in, trading at market-average PE despite double-digit growth. One quarter of cloud backlog decel triggered a 22% crash. The Nvidia partnership and consumption-based AI pricing could reaccelerate. Watch the next cloud backlog print.
items.tweetA: SAP: 90%+ of the Fortune 500. $700M switching costs. RPO up 92% in 3 years. Revenue growing for 5 straight years at 9%. Stock down 37% from ATH. Why? Cloud backlog growth came in at 26% instead of 30%+. One print. That's SaaS multiple compression in real time. Is the cloud backlog bottom in? That's the whole trade.
items.tweetB: Jensen Huang just called out SAP as a key enterprise AI agent partner. SAP's 'Joule' AI replaces seat-based licensing with consumption pricing — pay for outcomes, not users. If that monetization model works, this is a 30%+ compounder at 19.8x forward PE. If cloud backlog keeps decelerating, it stays a value trap.

items.title: Correlation Index (COR1M) at 6 — AI crowding so extreme the index has lost its hedge properties
items.idea: The S&P Correlation Index (COR1M) closed at 6 — below the 8 threshold indicating highly dispersed positioning. Traders are aggressively buying upside calls in a handful of AI leaders, creating massive positive Gamma Exposure (GEX) in specific names that move independently of the broader market. Implied correlation of the entire index collapses. Below 8 means the index is not behaving like a diversified instrument.
items.angle: COR1M at 6 means buying SPX puts is cheap because vol is suppressed across 490+ non-AI names. The hedge is effectively subsidized by the crowding. This is the cheapest macro hedge available: buy index puts when everyone is long individual AI names.
items.tweetA: The Correlation Index (COR1M) just closed at 6. Below 8 = positioning is historically dispersed. Translation: everyone is long Nvidia and a few AI names — but nobody is long the index as a portfolio. The index vol is cheap because it's representing 5 stocks. SPX puts are mispriced. Structurally.
items.tweetB: When COR1M hits 6, 'the market' has become a handful of AI stocks with 490 passengers. The S&P 500 is not a diversified instrument right now. It's five stocks with tail protection on the other 495. If those five correct together — and they will — the index protection you thought you had does nothing.

items.title: SpaceX IPO pricing June 11-12: $75B raise, $1.5T+ valuation — 3x the largest US IPO in history
items.idea: SpaceX targeting June 11-12 for IPO pricing at $1.5T+ valuation, making it a $75B raise — 3x larger than the previous largest US IPO ever. CNBC reports Tesla and SpaceX merger talks ongoing; combined entity would be the world's 5th-largest corporate Bitcoin treasury ($3.3B). Alphabet independently planning to raise $80B. Between SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic, and Alphabet, equity issuance pipeline over next 60-90 days is historically unprecedented.
items.angle: June 11-12 is the most important IPO pricing event since dot-com. SpaceX at $1.5T sets the risk premium for Anthropic ($900B) and OpenAI ($730B). If SpaceX prices weak, both AI IPOs reset lower. Treat it as a macro event.
items.tweetA: SpaceX prices June 11-12. $75B raise. $1.5T valuation. 3x the largest US IPO in history. The outcome of that single day sets the risk premium for Anthropic at $900B, OpenAI at $730B, and every other AI unicorn waiting in line. June 11 is a macro event, not just an IPO.
items.tweetB: Tesla + SpaceX merger talks per CNBC. Combined entity world's 5th-largest corporate Bitcoin treasury at $3.3B. Alphabet raising $80B. SpaceX $75B. Anthropic and OpenAI TBD. The equity issuance pipeline for summer 2026: de-equitisation era is categorically over.

items.title: Gold at $4,535 falls 1.25% while PCE hits 3.8% and equities hit records — inflation hedge is broken
items.idea: Gold futures fell 1.25% to $4,535.80 on the same day all three US equity indexes closed at records and April PCE printed 3.8% YoY (highest since 2023). Gold is supposed to rise with inflation and as a hedge vs risk assets — but both conditions are present and gold is losing. This mirrors Charlie Morris's observation: everything (value stocks, bonds, gold, Bitcoin) is being liquidated to fund semiconductor positions. The correlation breakdown is positioning-driven, not fundamental.
items.angle: Two trades in one: selling gold to fund semis = when semis peak, gold gets capital back at the same time inflation is still 3.8%. Long gold / short semis is the same bet expressed two ways.
items.tweetA: Gold is at $4,535. Down 1.25% today. On the same day PCE printed 3.8%. Normally gold goes up when inflation is high. Normally gold goes up when stocks are at records (hedge). Today: both happened and gold fell. This is not a fundamental move. It's a positioning move. Everything is being sold to buy semiconductors.
items.tweetB: PCE 3.8%. Gold $4,535, -1.25%. S&P record high. Simple explanation: gold bulls are forced sellers funding Nvidia positions. When the semiconductor trade reverses, gold gets its capital back fast — at the same time inflation is still 3.8%. That's the long gold / short semis trade in one sentence.
